title: Cryptographic erasure attestation — per-asset destruction evidence
exclusions: Does NOT evaluate media-destruction workflows; focuses on cryptographic-key destruction as the disposal method.
evidence: Saved search 'crypto_erasure_attestation' (daily) intersects asset-disposal events against KMS key-destruction events.

controlTest.positiveScenario: An asset destruction event for host host-01 has no matching KMS key-destruction event within 24h; the UC fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Matching KMS key-destruction event within 24h; no alert.

dataSources: Asset disposal workflow events, KMS key destruction events.
spl:
index=assets sourcetype=asset:disposal earliest=-7d method=cryptographic_erasure
| join asset_id [search index=kms action=DeleteKey earliest=-8d | fields asset_id key_id _time | rename _time as key_deleted_at]
| eval delta=abs(_time-key_deleted_at)
| where isnull(key_deleted_at) OR delta>86400
| table asset_id key_id _time key_deleted_at delta
description: Correlates asset disposal events with the matching KMS key-destruction events to provide verifiable erasure evidence.
value: Solves the 'how do I prove we actually erased that data?' question that every DPO faces.
implementation: (1) Tag asset-disposal events with asset_id and key_id; (2) ingest KMS DeleteKey; (3) run daily. Indexes required: `index=assets`, `index=kms`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=asset:disposal`.
visualization: Table of orphan disposal events, single-value '% of disposals with matched key destruction'. Key fields: `asset_id`, `delta`, `key_deleted_at`, `key_id`.
knownFalsePositives: Retention overruns during scheduled retention cycles, legal hold changes, or platform migrations.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Asset disposal workflow events, KMS key destruction events..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Tag asset-disposal events with asset_id and key_id; (2) ingest KMS DeleteKey; (3) run daily.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=assets sourcetype=asset:disposal earliest=-7d method=cryptographic_erasure
| join asset_id [search index=kms action=DeleteKey earliest=-8d | fields asset_id key_id _time | rename _time as key_deleted_at]
| eval delta=abs(_time-key_deleted_at)
| where isnull(key_deleted_at) OR delta>86400
| table asset_id key_id _time key_deleted_at delta
```

Understanding this SPL

**Cryptographic erasure attestation — per-asset destruction evidence** — Solves the 'how do I prove we actually erased that data?' question that every DPO faces.

Documented **Data sources**: Asset disposal workflow events, KMS key destruction events. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: assets; **sourcetype**: asset:disposal. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=assets, sourcetype=asset:disposal, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **delta** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(key_deleted_at) OR delta>86400` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: _time, asset_id, delta, key_deleted_at, key_id.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the records, archive, or legal-hold system and compare with the GRC system so retention and disposal match policy.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of orphan disposal events, single-value '% of disposals with matched key destruction'.
grandmaExplanation: We help you see retention and disposal so data is not kept too long and holds are respected.
